description: Best-in-class design token platform — inline editing, rich previews, token engine enhancements, multi-dimensional theming, deep Figma integration, platform export, and developer handoff tools.

userStories.title: Inline Token Value Editing — Infrastructure
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to click directly on a token value in the token list to edit it inline, so that I don't have to navigate to a separate editor screen for simple value changes.
acceptanceCriteria: InlineEditor.tsx component exists at packages/figma-plugin/src/ui/components/InlineEditor.tsx
Single-clicking a token's value cell in the list activates inline editing for that token
The inline input is pre-populated with the current $value (stringified)
Pressing Enter saves via PATCH API and refreshes the list
Pressing Escape cancels editing and restores the original value
Clicking outside the input cancels editing (blur handler)
Only one token can be inline-edited at a time (activating a new one cancels the previous)
Double-click on the token row (non-value area) still opens the full TokenEditor
Toast shows 'Token updated' on successful save
Toast shows error message on failed save
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inline Editor — Color Picker Variant
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see a mini color picker popover when I inline-edit a color token, so that I can visually pick colors without leaving the token list.
acceptanceCriteria: Color tokens show a HexColorPicker popover when inline editing is activated
The hex input field is editable alongside the visual picker
The list swatch updates in real-time as the picker changes
Saving is debounced (300ms) during drag, immediate on blur/Enter/Escape
Popover positions itself to avoid clipping at window edges
Popover closes on Escape, Enter, or click-outside
Non-color tokens still use the base text input
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inline Editor — Dimension & Number Variants
userStories.description: As a designer, I want inline editing of dimension tokens to show a number input with a unit dropdown, and number tokens to show a plain number input.
acceptanceCriteria: Dimension tokens show number input + unit dropdown inline
Unit dropdown contains all units from DIMENSION_UNITS in packages/core/src/constants.ts
Number tokens show a plain number input
Arrow Up/Down increments/decrements by 1
Shift+Arrow changes by 10, Alt+Arrow by 0.1
Enter saves, Escape cancels
Value is saved in DTCG format: { value: number, unit: string } for dimension, number for number
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reference/Alias Autocomplete Dropdown
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a fuzzy-search autocomplete dropdown when I type { in any token value field, so that I can quickly find and select token references without memorizing paths.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenAutocomplete.tsx exists at packages/figma-plugin/src/ui/components/TokenAutocomplete.tsx
Typing { in the inline editor activates the autocomplete dropdown
Typing { in the full TokenEditor's value/reference field activates it
Fuzzy search matches on token path segments (e.g., pri.5 matches color.primary.500)
Results are filtered by compatible $type when a filter type is provided
Each suggestion shows: preview swatch/icon, token path, set name, resolved value
Arrow Up/Down navigates suggestions, Enter selects, Escape dismisses
Selecting a suggestion inserts {path.to.token} into the input field
Dropdown shows max 8 items with scroll for more
Dropdown positions below (or above if near bottom edge) the input
Works in both InlineEditor and TokenEditor contexts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rich Token Preview — Typography
userStories.description: As a designer, I want typography tokens in the token list to show a live text preview rendered with the actual font family, size, weight, and line-height.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenPreview.tsx exists at packages/figma-plugin/src/ui/components/TokenPreview.tsx
Typography tokens in the list show a rendered 'Aa' text sample
The sample uses the correct font-family, font-weight, letter-spacing from the token value
Font size in the preview is capped at 16px to fit the list row
A summary label shows fontFamily fontSize/lineHeight fontWeight (e.g., 'Inter 14/20 500')
Alias typography tokens resolve to the final value before rendering
Non-typography tokens are not affected
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rich Token Preview — Shadow
userStories.description: As a designer, I want shadow tokens in the token list to show a small card element with the actual shadow applied.
acceptanceCriteria: Shadow tokens show a small card with the actual shadow applied
Drop shadows and inner shadows render correctly
The CSS box-shadow is derived from the DTCG shadow value fields
Alias shadow tokens resolve before rendering
A text label shows the offset/blur values
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rich Token Preview — Gradient
userStories.description: As a designer, I want gradient tokens to show a gradient swatch bar in the token list.
acceptanceCriteria: Gradient tokens show a horizontal gradient swatch in the list
The gradient uses the token's color stops and positions
Alias gradient tokens resolve before rendering
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rich Token Preview — Dimension, Border, Duration, Boolean
userStories.description: As a designer, I want visual previews for dimension, border, duration, and boolean tokens in the list.
acceptanceCriteria: Dimension tokens show a proportional bar + value label
Border tokens show a styled rectangle with the border applied
Number tokens in 0-1 range show a fill bar
Boolean tokens show a toggle icon reflecting true/false
Duration tokens show the value with unit suffix
All previews resolve aliases before rendering
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rich Token Preview — Integration into TokenList
userStories.description: As a designer, I want all rich token previews to replace the current generic value display in the token list, unified under a single TokenPreview component.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenList.tsx uses <TokenPreview> for all token value displays
Existing color swatch rendering is consolidated into TokenPreview
All token types (color, typography, shadow, gradient, dimension, border, number, boolean, duration, composition, string) have a preview or fallback
Previews fit within the existing row height
No visual regression for color tokens (swatches look the same)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Safe Token Rename — Server API
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to rename a token path and have all alias references across all sets automatically update.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/tokens/rename endpoint exists
Without confirm: true, returns dry-run preview with all affected tokens listed
With confirm: true, renames the token and updates all references across all sets
References in expressions (e.g., {old.path} * 2) are also updated
Group renames update all children and their references
Returns 400 if toPath already exists (no silent overwrite)
Returns 404 if fromPath doesn't exist
On failure, no partial changes are persisted
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Safe Token Rename — Plugin UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a rename modal that shows me which tokens will be affected before I confirm a rename.
acceptanceCriteria: Rename action opens a modal with new path input
Modal shows a dry-run list of all affected references
Each affected reference shows: set name, token path, old value → new value
Confirm button executes the rename
Token list refreshes after successful rename
Toast shows 'Renamed [old] → [new], updated X references'
Cancel button dismisses without changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Gradient Token Type — Visual Editor
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create and edit gradient tokens with a visual editor that shows angle, color stops, and positions.
acceptanceCriteria: GradientEditor component exists
Gradient preview bar renders the current gradient
Color stops are draggable to change position
Clicking a stop opens a color picker
Clicking empty space adds a new stop at that position
Dragging a stop off the bar removes it (if more than 2 stops remain)
Angle input stored in $extensions.angle (default: 180deg)
The value saves as DTCG GradientValue format
New gradient tokens can be created with type gradient
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Cubic Bezier Token Type — Easing Curve Editor
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create and edit cubic-bezier tokens with a visual curve editor showing control point handles.
acceptanceCriteria: CubicBezierEditor component exists
Canvas shows the bezier curve with two draggable control points
Dragging control points updates the curve in real time
Numeric inputs for x1, y1, x2, y2 are shown and editable
Preset buttons (ease, ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out, linear) set known values
A small animation preview dot shows the easing behavior
Value saves as [x1, y1, x2, y2] array
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Duration Token Type — Editor & Preview
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create and edit duration tokens with a value + unit (ms/s) input.
acceptanceCriteria: Duration tokens in TokenEditor show number input + ms/s dropdown
Value saves as DTCG DurationValue: { value: number, unit: 'ms' | 's' }
Inline editing also works for duration tokens
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Composition Token Visual Editor
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a visual editor for composition tokens that lets me assemble multiple property values into named slots.
acceptanceCriteria: Composition tokens show a multi-slot editor with property name + value rows
Each value input supports the TokenAutocomplete dropdown
Users can add and remove property slots
A live preview div shows the composed styles applied
Predefined slot group buttons add common property sets
Value saves as Record<string, string | number> in DTCG format
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Multi-Dimensional Theming — Data Model
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to organize themes into dimensions (e.g., Brand, Mode, Density) so that I can create combinable theme axes.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeDimension and ThemeDimensionValue types exist in types.ts
GET /api/themes/dimensions returns dimension data
POST /api/themes/dimensions creates/updates a dimension with values and set configs
POST /api/themes/dimensions returns 400 if creating would exceed 4 dimensions or 10 values per dimension
DELETE /api/themes/dimensions/:name removes a dimension
GET /api/themes/resolve accepts dimension value selections and returns merged set config
Existing flat $themes data still works (backward compatible)
Dimensions are persisted in $themes.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Multi-Dimensional Theming — Plugin UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create and manage theme dimensions in the Themes tab, and preview any combination.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeManager shows a 'Dimensions' section when dimensions exist
Users can create a dimension with a name
Users can add values to a dimension and configure set statuses per value
UI warns when total combinations exceed 50
UI prevents adding more than 4 dimensions or 10 values per dimension
A 'Preview' section has one dropdown per dimension
Selecting values shows the resolved set configuration
Legacy flat themes still display and function
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Set Ordering — Drag-to-Reorder in Theme Editor
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to drag sets in the theme editor to change their override priority.
acceptanceCriteria: Each set row in the theme editor has a drag handle
Dragging a set reorders it in the visual list
The ordering is persisted to the server when changed
A label or tooltip explains that higher sets override lower sets
The token resolver respects the persisted order (last enabled set with a matching path wins)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Resizable Plugin Window
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to resize the Figma plugin window so that I can see more tokens at once on larger screens.
acceptanceCriteria: A resize handle is visible in the bottom-right corner of the plugin
Dragging the handle resizes the plugin window
Minimum size: 320x400
Maximum size: 800x900
The last used size is saved to figma.clientStorage
On plugin open, the window restores to the last used size
The layout adapts without breaking at all supported sizes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Used in Figma — Layer Count Badges
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see how many Figma layers reference each token to identify unused tokens and understand token adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: Controller handles 'scan-token-usage' message with optional scope param
Default: scans current page. With scope: 'all-pages': scans all pages with progress reporting
Controller returns token path → count map
Token list shows a usage count badge next to each token
Tokens with 0 references show a distinct visual (muted/dim badge)
An 'Unused' filter toggle shows only tokens with 0 references
A refresh button re-triggers the scan
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Used in Figma — Layer Detail Popover
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to click the usage badge to see which specific layers use a token and click a layer name to select it on the canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the usage badge opens a popover with layer names
Each layer name is clickable
Clicking a layer name selects it on the Figma canvas and scrolls to it
The popover shows up to 20 layers, with a '+N more' indicator if truncated
Popover closes on click-outside or Escape
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Variable Scope Management — Editor UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to set Figma variable scopes when editing a token so that variables are only suggested for appropriate properties.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenEditor shows a 'Figma Scopes' section for applicable token types (color, number, dimension, boolean)
Checkboxes are shown for all Figma variable scope categories
Selected scopes are saved to $extensions.figmaScopes on the token
Scope overrides are persisted in the server config
When syncing to Figma, the controller applies scopes to created/updated variables
Import from Figma preserves existing variable scopes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diff-from-Figma Badges
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see which tokens have diverged between my local token files and the current Figma variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Controller handles 'compute-figma-diff' and returns per-token diff status
Diverged tokens show a colored dot indicator in the list
Hovering/clicking the dot shows local vs Figma values
'Show diverged only' filter toggle works
'Accept all Figma values' bulk action updates local tokens via server API
'Push all local values' bulk action triggers sync-to-figma for diverged tokens
Diff computation runs in background after connection (non-blocking)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Live CSS/Code Output Preview Panel
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a side panel showing the generated CSS/SCSS/JSON output in real-time as I edit tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: CodePreview.tsx exists at packages/figma-plugin/src/ui/components/CodePreview.tsx
A toggle or tab activates the code preview panel within the Tokens view
CSS, SCSS, JSON, and TypeScript formats are selectable
Output updates in real-time when tokens change
Copy-to-clipboard button copies the entire output
'Show resolved' vs 'Show aliases' toggle works
Output is syntax-highlighted with colored tokens
The panel is scrollable for large token sets
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Platform-Specific Export — Configuration UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to configure export targets (Web CSS, iOS Swift, Android XML, React Native, Flutter Dart) with naming conventions and value transforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Each platform in ExportPanel has an expandable configuration section
Naming convention dropdown with kebab-case, camelCase, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, lowerCamelCase
Color format options: hex, rgba, UIColor (iOS), Color() (Compose), Color (Flutter)
Dimension format options: px, dp, pt, rem, sp
Output preview shows a sample of 10 lines in the configured format
Configurations are saved to server config for persistence
'Quick Export' re-runs the last used configuration
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Platform-Specific Export — Server Transforms
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the server to generate correctly-formatted output files for each platform target.
acceptanceCriteria: Transform functions exist for CSS, SCSS, iOS Swift, Android XML, Compose, React Native, Flutter, JSON
POST /api/export/:platform endpoint returns formatted output (content only)
POST /api/export/:platform/write endpoint writes files to the configured output directory
Output directory defaults to {tokenDir}/../build/ and is configurable in server config (exportOutputDir)
Color values are correctly converted per platform format
Dimension values are correctly converted per platform format
Token paths are renamed per naming convention config
All aliases are resolved in exported output
Typography tokens export as structured objects (or platform-appropriate constructs)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Custom Export Templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to define custom export templates using Handlebars syntax to generate output formats specific to my project.
acceptanceCriteria: handlebars is a dependency in packages/server/package.json
POST /api/export/custom renders a Handlebars template with token data
Built-in helpers: kebabCase, camelCase, hexToRgba, pxToRem
Templates can be saved to and loaded from server config
The Export panel has a 'Custom' tab with a template editor and live preview
Invalid templates return a clear error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Developer Documentation Portal — Server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to browse the design token documentation at localhost:9400/docs in my browser without needing Figma.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/server/src/routes/docs.ts exists
Visiting localhost:9400/docs in a browser shows the documentation portal
When server is started with --auth, the docs route requires basic auth
Sidebar shows all token sets, expandable to groups
Tokens display with visual previews (color swatches, typography samples)
Search bar filters tokens by path, value, or description
Filter by token type works
Token detail view shows: resolved value, raw alias, description, deprecation status
Dark/light mode toggle
No Figma plugin or build step required
Responsive layout (works on mobile screens)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Token Changelog — Git History in Plugin
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see recent token changes inside the plugin so I can track what changed and when without switching to a terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/sync/changelog?limit=50 endpoint exists and returns structured changelog data
The endpoint parses git history and diffs token files
The Sync tab shows a 'History' section with recent changes
Each commit is expandable to show individual token changes
Token changes show path, action (created/updated/deleted), and value diff
Color changes show side-by-side swatches
Default limit is 50 commits, configurable via ?limit=N query param
UI shows a 'Load more' button that fetches the next 50 commits
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Changed Since Last Sync Badges
userStories.description: As a designer, I want tokens that have changed since the last Figma sync to show a badge so I know what needs to be re-synced.
acceptanceCriteria: Last sync timestamp is saved to figma.clientStorage after each sync
Server endpoint GET /api/sync/changed-since?timestamp=ISO returns tokens modified after the given time
Changed tokens show an orange dot badge in the list
'Changed since sync' filter toggle works
Sync panel header shows 'X tokens changed' count
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Find & Replace — Server Batch API
userStories.description: As a backend component, the server needs a batch update endpoint for find & replace operations.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/tokens/batch-update endpoint exists
Accepts an array of updates with setName, path, and new $value
All updates are applied atomically
Returns count of updated tokens and any errors
Invalid paths return errors without blocking other updates
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Find & Replace — Search UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to search tokens by value or reference pattern and replace them in bulk.
acceptanceCriteria: FindReplace.tsx component exists at packages/figma-plugin/src/ui/components/FindReplace.tsx
Search by literal value finds all tokens with matching $value
Search by reference finds all tokens whose value contains {searchPath}
Search by name pattern matches token paths via regex
Results show set name, token path, current value with preview
'Preview' shows before/after without applying
'Replace All' applies changes to all matches
Individual 'Replace' applies to a single match
Toast confirms 'Replaced X tokens'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Keyboard Navigation — Token List
userStories.description: As a power user, I want to navigate the token list and open editors entirely via keyboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow Up/Down moves focus between token rows
The focused row has a visible focus indicator (ring or background highlight)
The focused row scrolls into view when navigating
Enter activates inline editing
Cmd+E opens the full editor
Escape closes the inline editor or deselects
Delete/Backspace triggers delete confirmation for selected tokens
Space toggles multi-selection
Cmd+A selects all visible tokens
Keyboard navigation works when the token list has focus
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keyboard Shortcuts — Global
userStories.description: As a power user, I want global keyboard shortcuts for common actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+F focuses the search bar
Cmd+Shift+F opens Find & Replace
Cmd+S saves the current edit
Cmd+N opens new token form
Default browser behavior is prevented for captured shortcuts
Tooltips on buttons show the keyboard shortcut
A 'Keyboard Shortcuts' help panel lists all shortcuts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Copy-to-Clipboard Enhancements
userStories.description: As a designer/developer, I want to quickly copy token paths, CSS variable names, or values to my clipboard in various formats.
acceptanceCriteria: A copy icon appears on token row hover
Clicking it shows a menu with format options
'Copy path' copies the dot-delimited path
'Copy as CSS' copies var(--kebab-path)
'Copy as SCSS' copies $kebab-path
'Copy as JS' copies a valid JS property access expression
'Copy as reference' copies {dot.path}
'Copy value' copies the resolved value
Toast confirms copy
Multi-select: 'Copy as JSON' copies all selected tokens
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configurable Server URL
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to change the server URL from within the plugin so I can connect to a different port or a remote server.
acceptanceCriteria: A settings UI is accessible from the plugin (gear icon or settings section)
Server URL input field defaults to http://localhost:9400
Entering just a number uses http://localhost:{number}
'Test Connection' button calls the health endpoint and shows result
Connection status (green/red) updates based on test result
The URL is persisted in figma.clientStorage
On plugin open, the saved URL is loaded before connecting
Invalid URLs show a validation error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Token Usage Analytics — Dashboard
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want a dashboard showing token health metrics: unused tokens, orphan references, alias chain depth, and most-used tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tokens/analytics endpoint exists and returns the analytics data
unusedTokens correctly identifies tokens with no incoming references
orphanReferences correctly identifies broken alias references
aliasChainDepth computes max and average depth
tokenCountByType and tokenCountBySet are accurate
mostReferenced returns top 10 tokens sorted by reference count
healthScore is computed (0-100) based on orphans, deprecation, and description coverage
AnalyticsPanel.tsx displays all metrics as stat cards
Orphan references are listed with their broken target paths
Unused tokens are listed with their set and type
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Reset Plugin — Clear All Data
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a way to reset the plugin completely and remove all stored data, so I can start fresh or troubleshoot issues without reinstalling.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reset Plugin' option is accessible from the settings panel
Clicking it shows a confirmation dialog listing what will be cleared: clientStorage (server URL, last sync timestamp, window size, all cached state)
Confirming clears all figma.clientStorage keys set by the plugin
After reset, the plugin reloads to its initial state (as if opened for the first time)
The confirmation dialog requires a deliberate action (e.g., typing 'RESET' or clicking a second confirm button) to prevent accidental data loss
Toast or inline message confirms 'Plugin data cleared'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
